articles.title: Artemis II’s Moon flyby turns a test flight into a live lunar science rehearsal
articles.summary: NASA’s first crewed lunar mission in more than half a century is doing more than carrying astronauts around the Moon. As Orion loops the far side, the crew is practicing observations, mission-control choreography, and space-weather monitoring that NASA says will shape the next generation of lunar exploration.

articles.title: The AI industry’s new arms race is not just about models — it’s about compute, control and trust
articles.summary: As April 2026 opens, the biggest AI stories are less about flashy demos than the industrial base behind them: giant compute deals, enterprise guardrails and a scramble to make agents useful without making them unsafe.

articles.title: Google’s April AI push shows the race has shifted from bigger models to more useful ones
articles.summary: In a fast-moving April, Google has been using Gemini, Search Live, Maps, Workspace and creator tools to make AI feel less like a demo and more like a layer inside everyday software.

articles.title: Why birding is having a bigger moment than many people realize
articles.summary: A once-quiet pastime has become a brain-healthy, citizen-science-powered obsession, and new research helps explain why more Americans are reaching for binoculars.

articles.title: Taiwan’s opposition leader’s Beijing trip tests the island’s political center as military pressure rises
articles.summary: Cheng Li-wun’s meeting with Xi Jinping comes as Taipei struggles over defense spending, Washington worries about deterrence, and Beijing seeks to turn cross-strait politics into its own diplomatic advantage.

articles.title: Pakistan tests whether the Iran ceasefire can become a deal
articles.summary: In Islamabad, U.S. and Iranian negotiators are trying to turn a fragile two-week pause into a durable end to a war that has already reshaped Middle East diplomacy and rattled energy markets.

articles.title: Trump’s tariffs are back in court, and the fight is really about presidential power
articles.summary: A federal trade court heard a fresh challenge to the White House’s latest global tariffs, reviving a broader constitutional battle over whether presidents can tax imports by emergency decree.

articles.title: As tariffs loomed, March inflation and jobs data showed a U.S. economy still balancing growth and price pressure
articles.summary: The latest CPI and employment figures suggest the American economy entered the IMF and World Bank spring meetings with enough momentum to avoid a clean slowdown, but not enough clarity to dismiss the risk that trade policy and persistent price gains will keep monetary policy on edge.

articles.title: Artemis II’s Moon Flyby Marks a New Test for Human Deep-Space Exploration
articles.summary: As NASA’s first crewed lunar mission in more than half a century circles the Moon, the flight is offering a rare live test of spacecraft systems, human physiology and lunar science planning.

articles.title: OpenAI’s April surge shows the AI race has moved from models to distribution, operations and trust
articles.summary: A wave of releases, partnerships and policy messaging in early April suggests the defining AI battle of 2026 is no longer just about who has the smartest model—but who can turn it into a durable product, a trusted platform and an enterprise habit.
